title: NIS2 Art.31-35 supervision, enforcement, fine exposure and GDPR coordination evidence

dataSources: `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:audit_finding` with `finding_id`, `due_date`, `competent_authority`, `remediation_status`; `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` (policy attestations / supervisory tasks); Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)); `index=privacy` optional GDPR breach correlation with `breach_id`, `dpo_signoff`; evidence landing reviews in `index=audit_evidence`.
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)); Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)) optional for incident linkage.
spl:
(index=grc sourcetype=grc:audit_finding earliest=-60d)
       OR (index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy earliest=-60d)
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval supervision_gap=if(sourcetype="grc:audit_finding" AND remediation_status IN ("open","in_progress") AND isnotnull(due_epoch) AND now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| eval policy_gap=if(sourcetype="snow:sn_grc_policy" AND state!="6" AND match(short_description,"(?i)supervisory|inspect|nis2"),1,0)
| where supervision_gap=1 OR policy_gap=1
| eval row_key=coalesce(finding_id,number)
| lookup nis2_supervision_kfp_exceptions row_key OUTPUT exception_until
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table _time finding_id competent_authority due_date remediation_status number short_description supervision_gap policy_gap
description: Surfaces overdue supervisory audit findings and non-closed ServiceNow GRC policy tasks whose descriptions reference inspections or articles so legal can see evidence request backlogs before penalties or GDPR overlaps escalate.
value: Executives maintain an operational cockpit for authority interactions—response SLAs, open remediation, and linked privacy incidents—without claiming that any Splunk panel replaces counsel’s interpretation of fines or jurisdiction.
implementation: (1) Land `grc:audit_finding` via your GRC tool’s connector. (2) Map ServiceNow policy table through Splunkbase 1928. (3) Optional: ingest DPO breach tickets to `index=privacy`. (4) Daily alert; route to GC office. (5) Quarterly export to `index=audit_evidence`.
visualization: SLA breach table by `competent_authority`, timeline of tasks, heatmap of GDPR-tagged linked records.
knownFalsePositives:
• **Negotiated extension with regulator** — Distinguish: `finding_id` tagged `extension_granted` in GRC. Suppress: exception register with PDF hash reference.
• **Duplicate ServiceNow tasks after clone** — Distinguish: same `finding_id` twice. Suppress: `| dedup finding_id`.
• **Privacy breach record unrelated supervisory topic** — Distinguish: `breach_category` not crossing cyber incident. Suppress: filter `where breach_linkage="nis2_related"`.
• **Year-end closure codified offline** — Distinguish: counsel notes mailed; Splunk stale. Suppress: time-bound `lookup` until systems catch up.
exclusions: Does not compute fines, predict enforcement outcomes, or file formal responses; does not waive legal privilege; GDPR coordination is illustrative telemetry only unless your DPO adopts it.
evidence: Scheduled `UC-22.2.54` saved search output, underlying `grc:audit_finding` and `snow:sn_grc_policy` excerpts, optional `index=privacy` references, and immutable copy in `index=audit_evidence`.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Insert a synthetic `grc:audit_finding` event with `remediation_status=open` and `due_date` seven days in the past plus a matching `competent_authority` code, then confirm the search flags `supervision_gap=1` for that `finding_id` within the hourly run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: When findings are `closed` before the due date and there are no open `snow:sn_grc_policy` tasks matching the supervisory keyword filter, the production alert remains silent for an entire seven-day backtest window aside from known test fixtures kept in a dev index.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• GRC platform (ServiceNow GRC, Archer, etc.) streaming structured events to `index=grc` with stable `sourcetype=grc:audit_finding` mapping.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) configured for `sn_grc_policy` or equivalent; modular input interval 300 seconds, credentials in `passwords.conf`.
• Roles separating `privacy` data from general SOC; consider field masking.
• Lookup `nis2_competent_authorities.csv` for human-readable authority metadata (optional enrichment).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate API path such as `/api/now/table/sn_grc_policy` returns `number`, `short_description`, `state`, `due_date`. For GRC findings, ensure JSON includes `remediation_status`. If GDPR correlation needed, ingest DPA tickets with `breach_id`. Route all reviewer notes ONLY to `index=audit_evidence` with restrictive retention (example 7 years).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Schedule daily at 07:00 with a 60-day lookback for supervisory context.

```spl
(index=grc sourcetype=grc:audit_finding earliest=-60d)
OR (index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy earliest=-60d)
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval supervision_gap=if(sourcetype="grc:audit_finding" AND remediation_status IN ("open","in_progress") AND isnotnull(due_epoch) AND now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| eval policy_gap=if(sourcetype="snow:sn_grc_policy" AND state!="6" AND match(short_description,"(?i)supervisory|inspect|nis2"),1,0)
| where supervision_gap=1 OR policy_gap=1
```

Use `lookup nis2_supervision_kfp_exceptions` for counsel-approved holds. Throttle by `row_key=coalesce(finding_id,number)`.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick closed historical finding: ensure it does not appear. Open overdue sample from sandbox should match GRC UI. Cross-check ServiceNow record `state` codes versus lookup table.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Executive dashboard tile: count overdue by MS member state code. Webhook to legal slack channel with least data (`finding_id` only). Attach runbook describing when to escalate to outside counsel.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Null due dates** — default ingestion mapping; fix at source.
• **Regex too broad on tasks** — tune `match` expression after false positives; move keywords to lookup.
• **Index latency** — if `_indextime-_time` > 3600, increase poll frequency.
• **Privilege risk** — limit dashboard to `legal_ops` role only.
grandmaExplanation: We keep watch on official requests from regulators and the paperwork that answers them, so nothing important crosses its deadline quietly. We also help spot when a personal-data incident might touch the same storyline, so lawyers see both sides early.
dataModelAcceleration: Alerts CIM acceleration optional if you tag `grc:audit_finding`; otherwise rely on dedicated index partitioning.
